claim: Exactly proves the barrier contact derivative inequality on the pair-stabilized three-level family for w=12,16.
claim_boundary: This closes a certified slice of the final theorem, not the full multilevel Barrier Schur Compression Lemma.

theorem.name: Pair-stabilized contact derivative closure
theorem.proof: The negative violation barrier Gap=-B_w factors under A=u+v, B=u-v as Gap=(u-1)^2Q(u)+v^2R(u,v^2), with Q and R having strictly positive integer coefficients. For A,B>=0, u>=0 and v^2>=0, so Gap=0 implies u=1 and v=0. Thus A=B=1. Direct substitution gives D(B_w)=0.
theorem.remaining_global_gap: Extend from the pair-stabilized slice to arbitrary multilevel contact points, likely through Terwilliger/Krawtchouk block reduction or an SOS multiplier.
theorem.role: This closes the exact pair-stabilized contact slice of the Barrier Schur Compression Lemma.
theorem.statement: For the pair-stabilized three-level family x=(A,B,1,...,1), the barrier contact condition B_w=0 forces A=B=1, hence the pair-normal derivative D(B_w)=0 and the contact derivative inequality holds exactly.
